---
title: Namespace
description: Create isolated sub-caches with a shared adapter using key prefixes.
icon: FolderOpen
---

## What is a namespace?

A namespace is a scoped sub-cache built on top of the same adapter. Every key operation is automatically prefixed, so different namespaces cannot collide.

```ts
const userCache = cache.namespace("user");
const sessionCache = cache.namespace("session");

await userCache.set("1", userData);    // stored as "user:1"
await sessionCache.set("1", sessionData); // stored as "session:1"
```

## Creating a namespace

```ts
const ns = cache.namespace("prefix");

await ns.set("key", value, { ttl: "5m" });
const value = await ns.get("key");
await ns.delete("key");
await ns.has("key");
```

All `CacheInstance` methods are available on the namespace object.

## Custom separator

By default, the separator between prefix and key is `:`. Override it:

```ts
const ns = cache.namespace("user", { separator: "/" });
// keys become "user/key"
```

## Nested namespaces

Namespaces can be nested — each adds a prefix layer:

```ts
const org = cache.namespace("org:42");
const orgPosts = org.namespace("posts");

await orgPosts.set("1", post); // stored as "org:42:posts:1"
```

## Namespace plugin

For static namespaces applied at cache creation time, use the namespace plugin instead of calling `cache.namespace()`. See [Plugins](/docs/cache/plugins) for details.
